summary: ERR_MANIFEST_TIMEOUT thrown during restore against eu-west object storage
description: Restores against the eu-west cluster started failing with ERR_MANIFEST_TIMEOUT thrown from src/restore/fetcher.ts. The error names the manifest path and the configured deadline, thirty seconds, and it is tripping consistently rather than intermittently, unlike a normal transient blip.

comments.author: Priya Natarajan
comments.body: fetchWithDeadline aborts via the controller once HELIOS_MANIFEST_TIMEOUT_MS elapses and throws ERR_MANIFEST_TIMEOUT naming the path and the deadline, exactly what we're seeing. Checking object storage status for eu-west now.

comments.author: Sam Whitfield
comments.body: seeing the same on my end, thought it was just me

comments.author: Priya Natarajan
comments.body: Object storage in eu-west is degraded, response times for large objects are running four to five times normal. The thirty second deadline in src/restore/fetcher.ts was sized for typical latency and has no margin for this kind of regional slowdown.

comments.author: Priya Natarajan
comments.body: Storage provider confirmed the regional slowdown and gave an ETA for recovery, but rather than wait we raised HELIOS_MANIFEST_TIMEOUT_MS to 120 seconds for eu-west specifically. Restores are completing again, just slower than usual. Root cause is the slow object storage backend, not a bug in the fetch logic itself. Resolving, will revisit the deadline once the provider confirms recovery.

comments.author: Owen Reyes
comments.body: Confirmed restores succeeding again on my end. Thanks for the quick mitigation.
